text: The Trump administration has eased financial sanctions on Venezuela to help stabilize its struggling economy and address protests over low wages. The change allows major state banks, including the Banco Central de Venezuela, to use U.S. dollars, access oil revenues, and reconnect with the global financial system. Source: Axios

⚡ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Joint Israeli–Lebanese–U.S. statement Officials announced progress in Washington talks, outlining initial steps toward launching direct negotiations between Lebanon and Israel, with U.S. mediation: “Productive discussions were held on steps to launch direct negotiations. There is U.S. support for the Lebanese government’s plans to monopolize arms, and Washington hopes the talks will lead to a comprehensive peace agreement. Any Lebanese–Israeli ceasefire agreement will be under U.S. sponsorship and not through any separate track. The negotiations could lead to reconstruction and economic recovery for Lebanon. Israel supports the disarmament and dismantling of the infrastructure of armed groups in Lebanon, and it is committed to working with the Lebanese government on disarmament in a way that ensures the security of both countries. Lebanon affirms the principles of its territorial unity and its full sovereignty over its territory.” @wfwitness

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: Fwd from @ 📝 What Happened? 📝 On the Strait of Hormuz Blockade Despite announced blockade and control of the Strait of Hormuz by Donald Trump , the first results contradict expectations . Monitoring shows at least five vessels have passed through. 🔻 Whose Vessels Passed? ▪️ Confirmed: Rich Starry (China), Murlikishan (Madagascar), Peace Gulf (Panama) and Elpis (Comoros), and the bulk carrier Christianna (Liberia). Some previously faced US sanctions and worked with Iranian cargo. ▪️ Rich Starry, a Chinese vessel, departed UAE and entered the Gulf of Oman. ▪️ Peace Gulf is heading to UAE, Murlikishan to Iraq, not Iranian ports. ▪️ Elpis and Christianna departed Iranian ports after the blockade and passed through unhindered. 🖍 Estimates suggest up to 20 passages in 36 hours. Traffic has not been halted. 🚩 This occurs despite a dense US naval presence in the region, including the aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln . ❗️ Announcing a blockade is easier than enforcing it. Shipowners may take the risk if not calling at Iranian ports. 📌 The "blockade 2.0" seems more a show of presence than an actual closure - traffic continues, rules remain undefined. 📍 High resolution infographic 📍 English version #Iran #USA 🧨 @rybar_mena — on Middle Eastern chaos 💸

The incident may constitute a significant violation of U.S. and international sanctions on Russia. Ukraine demands Israel stop the ship from leaving. A Ukrainian court issued an arrest warrant for the vessel on 8 April. Today, Ukraine’s prosecutor general met Israel’s ambassador in Kyiv and requested international legal assistance to detain the ship. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 The IDF said it carried out overnight strikes on multiple Hezbollah infrastructures in the village of Adshit in southern Lebanon. Around 130 rockets have been launched from the village since the start of operations, targeting Israeli forces and areas including Kiryat Shmona. The strikes reportedly targeted weapons depots, launchers, and command centres, and the IDF said Hezbollah fighters were involved in planning attacks against Israel were killed. @wfwitness
